claim_bullets.bullet_text: Many programmers coming to JavaScript from other languages are familiar with three 'canonical' operations on collections: folding, filtering, and finding. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.175-176)
In Smalltalk, for example, they are known as collect , select , and detect . (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.175-176)
This is interesting, because it is lazy: It doesn't apply fn to every element in an iteration, just enough to find the first that passes the test. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.175-176)

source_record_text: Summary of the bonus chapter from JavaScript Allongé, focusing on lazy evaluation and collection operations in JavaScript.
